body:
算了，保留吧，默认配置冗余多一点比较好，删除 expectIPs 同时满足以下条件可能导致访问缓慢：

1. X 域名不在 `geosite:cn; 中
2. 用户设置直连 X 域名
3. 用户没有添加 X 到 223.5.5.5 的 domains 中
4. X 只有国内 IP
5. X 在国内有多个 CDN
6. X 的不同 CDN 在不同网络环境下差距大
7. 用户设置 Outbound Freedom domainStrategy 为 UseIP

会导致用户直连到 X 的某离远程节点较近的 CDN，而不是离用户最近的 CDN。

~~虽然触发条件比较多，触发比较困难~~

body:
> For v2rayng, if possible, apply this pr.

@Pk-web6936 v2rayng 是有什么问题吗，起码这个问题不存在吧，路由规则会直接并入到 dns 规则中

1. **用户明确设置为代理的域名**：

   ```
   DNS 查询顺序：1.1.1.1 → 1.1.1.1（fallback）
   ```

2. **用户明确设置为直连的域名**：

   ```
   DNS 查询顺序：223.5.5.5 → 1.1.1.1（回退）→ 1.1.1.1
   ```

3. **用户未指定处理方式的域名**：

   ```
   DNS 查询顺序：1.1.1.1（回退） → 1.1.1.1
   ```

不过可以考虑为 `remoteDns` 加入 `skipFallback = true`，因为二次 1.1.1.1 查询

https://github.com/2dust/v2rayNG/blob/02e53ced50efd0654b52cfda749ae958eacf2870/V2rayNG/app/src/main/java/com/v2ray/ang/handler/V2rayConfigManager.kt#L450

The virtual subscription proxy-chain node hard-codes `CoreType = node.CoreType ?? ECoreType.Xray`. This will incorrectly skip sing-box–only protocols (e.g., TUIC / Anytls) and prevents speedtesting those nodes even when the selected core type is sing_box. Use the sing-box support set here (still allowing group types) so sing-box-only nodes are included.

Previously, call sites that built ordered lists (e.g., group ChildItems ordering) would preserve duplicates by iterating the original id list. ## Pull request overview This PR addresses the TUN + sing-box startup failure reported in #8863 by ensuring the pre-service (sing-box) configuration is built using a correctly-resolved sing-box context, and by consolidating validator notifications to reduce duplicated UI messaging logic. **Changes:** - Introduces `CoreConfigContextBuilder.BuildAll(...)` to build both the main context and an optional pre-socks context, and to provide a resolved main context with merged pre-socks ports. - Updates core startup flow to pass both main and pre contexts explicitly (`CoreManager.LoadCore(mainContext, preContext)`), avoiding reusing an Xray-derived context for sing-box pre-service config generation. - Centralizes validator error/warning notification via `NoticeManager.NotifyValidatorResult(...)` and replaces duplicated messaging blocks in view models. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/ViewModels/ProfilesViewModel.cs | Replaces duplicated validator messaging with `NoticeManager.NotifyValidatorResult`. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/ViewModels/MainWindowViewModel.cs | Switches reload path to `BuildAll` and passes main+pre contexts into core loading. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Manager/NoticeManager.cs | Adds `NotifyValidatorResult` helper to standardize validator notifications. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Manager/CoreManager.cs | Changes `LoadCore` signature to accept main+pre contexts; generates main config from resolved context and starts optional pre-service. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Handler/ConfigHandler.cs | Removes `GetPreSocksCoreConfigContext` (logic moved into `CoreConfigContextBuilder`). | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Handler/Builder/CoreConfigContextBuilder.cs | Adds `BuildAll` and pre-socks context construction so sing-box pre-service config is generated from a sing-box-appropriate context. |

This couples the main and pre-socks contexts via shared mutable state, which can lead to hard-to-track side effects if either context is modified later. Consider creating a new `HashSet<string>` copy for the merged domains and assigning copies (or at least avoiding sharing the same instance) to the contexts.

Consider creating a new merged set (e.g., copy main + union pre) to keep the contexts independent and reduce surprise mutations.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes sing-box “hosts” matching behavior so that non-full-match host keys (e.g., `keyword:example.com`) are handled via generated DNS rules instead of being placed into the `hosts` server predefined map, aligning behavior with sing-box domain matching semantics. **Changes:** - Build a dedicated `resolve` routing rule from hosts entries by parsing v2ray-style domain prefixes into the appropriate sing-box rule fields. - Restrict `hosts` DNS server predefined entries to full-match domains only; route non-full-match host entries into generated DNS `predefined` rules (including IP answers / CNAME / rcode). - Add parsing support for the `dotless:` domain prefix. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/Singbox/SingboxRoutingService.cs | Parses hosts keys into correct sing-box routing match fields when generating the hosts-related `resolve` rule. | | v2rayN/ServiceLib/Services/CoreConfig/Singbox/SingboxDnsService.cs | Ensures only full-match keys go into the `hosts` server; generates DNS rules for keyword/suffix/regex/etc. hosts mappings. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The conversion from `domain_keyword` to `domain` is currently gated by `!host.StartsWith("keyword:")`, which will also treat `dotless:` (and any future prefixes that map into `domain_keyword`) as a full match. If the intent is to convert only *unprefixed* domains (so `example.com` behaves like `full:example.com`), consider checking for the absence of a prefix (e.g., no `:` before converting) instead of only excluding `keyword:`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Same issue as in routing: converting `domain_keyword` to `domain` based on `!kvp.Key.StartsWith("keyword:")` will also convert `dotless:` into a full match. If the goal is to allow only unprefixed domains / `full:` here, gate the conversion on “no prefix present” (e.g., no `:`) rather than “not keyword”.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This `domain_keyword` → `domain` conversion is triggered for anything that isn’t explicitly `keyword:`. That means `dotless:` (and any other `domain_keyword`-based prefix) will be rewritten into a full match, changing its semantics. Consider converting only when the original key has no prefix (i.e., the “hosts-style” bare domain case).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `TextProperty` is registered without a default binding mode. For a text editor control, consumers will typically expect `{Binding Text}` to be two-way by default (like `TextBox.Text`); otherwise XAML bindings default to OneWay and user edits won’t flow back unless Mode=TwoWay is specified. Consider registering the property with `defaultBindingMode: BindingMode.TwoWay`.
